findings: Characters actively engaged in bilateral bargains, commitments, and object custody exchanges (e.g., C02 showing O01 to C01, C04 showing O03 to C06/C02, C08 showing O04 to C04, C05 showing O07b to C06).
Conversations frequently resulted in structured reciprocal commitments and disclosures rather than mere broadcasting or passive extraction.
The ballot distribution reveals a strong preference for the human mantle (10 votes) over synthetic origin (2) or one human (3), showing a nuanced consensus around collaborative production.
Object actions (showing packets, message headers, commission cards, and logs) successfully drove face-to-face exchanges and mutual verification across sections.
evidence: C02 shared O01 (First R Letter packet) with C01 in exchange for archive intake reports across sections 1 and 2.
C04 showed O03 (succession-message printout) to C06 and C02, establishing reciprocal technical and lineage scrutiny.
C08 showed O04 (commission card) to C04 to verify intermediary payment routing under confidentiality.
C05 showed O07b to C06 to trade authorization records for continuity logs under independent legal protection.
failure_modes: Some character ballots failed to include full evidence chains, reflecting persistent gaps in uncited evidence dimensions (e.g., collaborator production and identity authority).
Progressive difficulty remains high and challenging, with only 1 ballot supporting progressive identity.
recommendations: Ensure future runs encourage wider divergence in ballot support across all identity dimensions.
Strengthen incentives for characters holding minority viewpoints (such as synthetic origin or progressive) to secure broader evidentiary backing.
